edibility: Inedible — too small and woody to be of culinary interest. The tough, cup-shaped fruiting bodies lack nutritional value and are not consumed.
taste: Not consumed due to woody texture and minimal edible mass.
search: Found in mulched garden beds, on decaying wood chips, and rotting logs during late summer through fall. Identify by distinctive cup-shaped fruiting bodies with striped (fluted) interior walls and bird's nest-like appearance with small egg-like peridioles inside. Can be confused with other bird's nest fungi like Crucibulum laeve, but C. striatus shows characteristic vertical striations.
